title: Workforms Checkbox Text
excerpt: Another day, another discovery of what Monday.com can’t do. I have a column/field in my form that is a checkbox, and when it’s displayed on the form there’s the column heading but no text I can put in line with the check…

posts.author: TempletonFerrari (Joe Public)
posts.cookedHtml:
Another day, another discovery of what Monday.com can’t do. I have a column/field in my form that is a checkbox, and when it’s displayed on the form there’s the column heading but no text I can put in line with the checkbox.

This is more pronounced the the true/false form field. It’s a true or false statement, but only has one checkbox and doesn’t allow any text. It makes no sense!

posts.author: LKat (Laura Katterhenry)
posts.cookedHtml:
For the True/False question in the form, you have the option of adding a title to your question and adding a description. The description is where you would make it clear what the check mark is indicating, as in:

Screenshot 2024-06-21 at 1.33.03 PM898×250 7.64 KB

If you want a better user experience, you may consider doing a status type question instead of a check box, and then when you’re editing the form, go to the status question settings and select “Show options in a list”, which will display them like this:

Hope that’s helpful!
